# CookieChimp Documentation

> CookieChimp is a developer-first Consent Management Platform (CMP) for GDPR, CCPA, and Google Consent Mode compliance. It intelligently blocks scripts until consent is granted.

---

# Overview

## What is CookieChimp?

CookieChimp is a modern, developer-first Consent Management Platform (CMP) that intelligently blocks scripts until consent is granted. It helps you seamlessly comply with GDPR, CCPA, Google Consent Mode and other privacy standards.

## What can CookieChimp do?

- **Cookie Management**: AI-powered cookie management for effortless compliance.
- **Consent Records**: Secure storage and management of user consent records.
- **API Access**: Full API access for managing consents and data integration.
- **Custom Widget**: Customisable consent interface with JavaScript and CSS options.
- **Headless Option**: Use as a headless platform with your own UI implementation.
- **Framework Agnostic**: Works with any web platform or framework.
- **GDPR & CCPA**: Automatic script blocking and opt-out options for compliance.
- **Privacy First**: Privacy-focused with data minimisation and security.

---

# How do I install CookieChimp?

Add CookieChimp to your website in under 5 minutes.

**Step 1: Add the JS code**

Add your website's CookieChimp JS snippet in the `<head>` tag of your HTML:

```html
<script src="https://cookiechimp.com/widget/abc123.js"></script>
```

Replace `abc123` with your website's unique CookieChimp ID.

> The CookieChimp script needs to be added at the top of the `<head>` section, so that it can run first, in order to ensure other scripts are only run based on consent.

**Step 2: Block Scripts & Cookies**

To ensure compliance with GDPR regulations, actively block all non-essential scripts and cookies until the user has given explicit consent. See the script blocking section below.

**Step 3: Allow users to update their preferences**

CookieChimp provides a floating privacy icon (Privacy Trigger) that allows users to manage their cookie preferences at any time. You can enable this in the banner settings.

Optionally, open the preferences modal with a custom button:

```html
<button type="button" data-cc="show-preferencesModal">
  Manage cookie preferences
</button>
```

## What if the banner doesn't show?

- Check if the domain is allowed in the Settings page on the platform.
- Ensure there are no conflicts with other scripts or styles on your website.
- Clear your browser cache and cookies, then reload the website.
- Enable "Debug mode" from the platform and open JS console to check for any errors.

---

# How do I block scripts until consent?

Control which scripts run based on user consent using data attributes.

> If you are using Google Tag Manager to trigger tags, see the Google Tag Manager section.

## What attributes can I use?

- `data-category`: Assigns the script to a consent category. Find the ID of each category from your CookieChimp dashboard.
- `data-service` (optional): Name of the company, service or a group of scripts. Can be used to disable a single service in a category while leaving others enabled.
- `data-type` (optional): Defines a custom script type.
- `data-src` (optional): Can be used instead of `src` for validation purposes.

## How do I block a script?

```html
<script>
  // Always executed
</script>

<script type="text/plain" data-category="analytics">
  // Executed when user consents to the "analytics" category
</script>

<script
  type="text/plain"
  data-category="analytics"
  data-service="Hotjar"
>
  // Executed when user consents to "Hotjar" service
</script>

<!-- Script loads when user consents to "Hotjar" service -->
<script
  type="text/plain"
  data-src="https://static.hotjar.com/c/hotjar-XXXXX.js?sv=X"
  data-category="analytics"
  data-service="Hotjar"
></script>
```

You can also run scripts when a category or service is disabled by prepending `!`:

```html
<script type="text/plain" data-category="!analytics">
  // Executed when the "analytics" category is disabled
</script>
```

---

# How do I embed a vendor list on my site?

Add an up-to-date vendor list to your cookie or privacy policy page.

Add the script to your `<head>`:

```html
<script src="https://cookiechimp.com/portal/{account_id}/vendors.js"></script>
```

Add the div where you want the vendor list:

```html
<div data-cookiechimp-vendor-list></div>
```

---

# FAQ

## How do I verify if everything is working correctly?

Go to "Settings" > "Consent Features" and enable Debug Mode. When a page view or consent is recorded, the banner will log the details in the browser console.

To ensure cookies are blocked and added on consent, use the developer console. Load your website after clearing cookies or in a private browser window. Check the "Storage" tab in the browser console to see if any cookies have been set.

## Why aren't CookieChimp cookies marked as HttpOnly?

`HttpOnly` cookies can only be used in server-side communication. Since the CookieChimp script is client-side code, in order for it to read these cookies, they cannot be marked as `HttpOnly`.

## Do we support cross-domain consent?

No. Cross Domain Consent requires third-party cookies, which many browsers do not allow.

However, we do support consent sharing across subdomains when the same CookieChimp script is installed. This is enabled by default.

## How can I obtain the consent ID?

The consent ID is shown in the preferences modal of the banner.

## How do I enable Global Privacy Control?

Go to the "Integrations" page and enable the Global Privacy Control integration. If a GPC signal is detected from the browser, opt-out preferences will automatically be set.

---

# How do I use CookieChimp with Google Tag Manager?

Add your website's CookieChimp JS snippet in the `<head>` tag of your HTML.

> Do not add the CookieChimp code via Google Tag Manager since we set the default consent states before Google Tag Manager runs.

```html
<script src="https://cookiechimp.com/widget/abc123.js"></script>
```

Replace `abc123` with your website's unique CookieChimp ID.

## How do I block tags with Google Tag Manager?

1. **Enable Google Consent Mode**: Navigate to "Integrations" from the sidebar and enable the Google Consent Mode integration.
2. **Add/Edit Tag**: Create or update a tag. Navigate to "Advanced Settings" > "Consent Settings". Check "Require additional consent for tag to fire" and add consent checks.
3. **Add a custom trigger**: Create a Custom Event trigger with event name `cookiechimp_consent_update`.

## How does CookieChimp map to Google consent types?

| CookieChimp Category ID | Google Consent Type                          |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| essential               | functionality_storage, security_storage      |
| marketing               | ad_storage, ad_user_data, ad_personalization |
| analytics               | analytics_storage                            |
| personalization         | personalization_storage                      |

When a user opts-in to a consent category, the linked Google consent type changes from 'denied' to 'granted'. We also send the consent state of individual services to Google.

---

# How do I add CookieChimp to Next.js?

Install CookieChimp in a Next.js application with the App Router or Pages Router.

## What do I need before starting?

- Next.js (App Router) project (`app/` directory).
- Your CookieChimp Account ID (replace `YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID` below).
- If testing locally: `localhost` added to Additional Domains in Account Settings > General.

## How do I load the CookieChimp script?

Use Next.js `<Script>` with `strategy="beforeInteractive"`:

```tsx
// app/layout.tsx
import Script from "next/script";

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <head>
        <Script
          src="https://cookiechimp.com/widget/YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID.js"
          strategy="beforeInteractive"
        />
      </head>
      <body>
        <div id="cookiechimp-container" />
        {children}
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

## How do I check consent status in code?

```tsx
// app/consent-logger.tsx
"use client";
import { useEffect } from "react";

export default function ConsentLogger() {
  useEffect(() => {
    function logConsent() {
      // @ts-ignore
      const allowed = window.CookieConsent?.acceptedService?.("Google Analytics", "analytics");
      if (allowed) {
        console.log("Google Analytics allowed");
      } else {
        console.log("Google Analytics blocked");
      }
    }

    logConsent();
    window.addEventListener("cc:onConsented", logConsent);
    window.addEventListener("cc:onUpdate", logConsent);

    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener("cc:onConsented", logConsent);
      window.removeEventListener("cc:onUpdate", logConsent);
    };
  }, []);

  return null;
}
```

Replace `"Google Analytics"` and `"analytics"` with the exact service and category names you configured in CookieChimp.

## How do I set this up with Pages Router?

Place the `<Script>` tag in `pages/_document.tsx` within `<Head>`, keep `<div id="cookiechimp-container" />` in `pages/_app.tsx`, and use the same ConsentLogger.

```tsx
// pages/_document.tsx
import { Html, Head, Main, NextScript } from 'next/document'
import Script from 'next/script'

export default function Document() {
  return (
    <Html>
      <Head>
        <Script
          src="https://cookiechimp.com/widget/YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID.js"
          strategy="beforeInteractive"
        />
      </Head>
      <body>
        <Main />
        <NextScript />
      </body>
    </Html>
  )
}
```

```tsx
// pages/_app.tsx
import type { AppProps } from 'next/app'

export default function App({ Component, pageProps }: AppProps) {
  return (
    <>
      <div id="cookiechimp-container" />
      <Component {...pageProps} />
    </>
  )
}
```

---

# How do I add CookieChimp to Shopify?

Add the CookieChimp Shopify app and set it up in your Shopify admin.

---

# How do I add CookieChimp to a Single Page Application?

Install CookieChimp in JavaScript-powered SPAs like React, Astro, and more.

## How do I load CookieChimp in an SPA?

Reinitialize the CookieChimp widget on page navigation:

```html
<script is:inline>
  function runCookieChimp() {
    var script = document.createElement("script");
    script.src = "https://cookiechimp.com/widget/K8ktWD.js";
    script.id = "cookiechimp-js";
    document.head.appendChild(script);
  }

  // for Astro JS
  document.addEventListener("astro:page-load", runCookieChimp);
</script>
```

## How do I position the Privacy Trigger in an SPA?

Place a div with ID `cookiechimp-container` in a location that does not get replaced during navigation:

```html
<div id="cookiechimp-container"></div>
```

For framework-specific persistence:

```html
<!-- For Turbo -->
<div id="cookiechimp-container" data-turbo-permanent></div>

<!-- For Astro JS -->
<div id="cookiechimp-container" transition:persist></div>
```

## How do I run code based on consent?

Use the `cc:onConsented` event:

```javascript
window.addEventListener("cc:onConsented", function (event) {
  if (CookieChimp.acceptedCategory("analytics")) {
    // "analytics" category enabled
  }

  if (CookieChimp.acceptedService("Google Analytics", "analytics")) {
    // "Google Analytics" service enabled
  }
});
```

Use the `cc:onUpdate` event for consent changes:

```javascript
window.addEventListener("cc:onUpdate", function (event) {
  var detail = event.detail;

  if (detail.changedCategories.includes("analytics")) {
    if (CookieChimp.acceptedCategory("analytics")) {
      // "analytics" category was just enabled
    } else {
      // "analytics" category was just disabled
    }

    if (detail.changedServices["analytics"].includes("Google Analytics")) {
      if (CookieChimp.acceptedService("Google Analytics", "analytics")) {
        // "Google Analytics" service was just enabled
      } else {
        // "Google Analytics" service was just disabled
      }
    }
  }
});
```

---

# How do I add CookieChimp to WordPress?

Install CookieChimp on your WordPress site with our plugin.

1. **Install CookieChimp WordPress Plugin**: Download from GitHub and upload to WordPress.
2. **Install WP Consent API plugin**: Install from the WordPress plugin directory. This allows other plugins to run tags based on consent.
3. **Configure CookieChimp Plugin**: Open plugin settings and enter your CookieChimp account ID.
4. **Enable WordPress Integration**: Enable the WordPress integration from the Integrations page.

---

# How does CookieChimp store consent records?

## How is visitor data stored?

- CookieChimp generates a random UUID for each visitor.
- No Personal Identifiable Information (PII) is stored in the database.

## How are IP addresses handled?

- IP addresses are processed only to determine visitor's country & region.
- IPs are masked before storage.
- Only country & region information is retained in logs.

## How do I find a user's consent record?

Search for specific consent records using the visitor's UUID, which can be found in the banner.

## How do I view consent details?

Click on consent records to see more details.

---

# How does vendor scanning work?

CookieChimp scans your website to find vendors and storage items used on your site. It detects services and storage items set before and after consent was granted.

Each service is flagged with one of these statuses:

- `Ran before consent` - A non-essential service ran before consent is granted.
- `Not shown in banner` - Storage items detected from a service not shown in your banner.
- `Not part of a category or service` - Storage items detected that cannot be matched to a vendor.
- `All good` - Everything is set up correctly.

## How do I run a manual scan?

Scans can be created from the Website Scan page. Free plans: 1 scan per day. Paid plans: 5 scans per day.

## How do I enable automatic scanning?

Enable auto scanning through the Vendor Scanner integration. Paid plans: automatic weekly scanning. Free plans: automatic monthly scanning.

---

# How do I manage vendors and storage items?

## What are categories?

Categories organize vendors into groups. Default categories: Essential, Analytics, Marketing, Personalization. A category becomes active when it contains an active service or storage items. Only active categories are shown to visitors in the banner.

## What are vendors?

Storage items set by a vendor/service can be grouped together. Services must be associated with a category. CookieChimp offers a vendor library of popular services. You can also create services manually.

## What are storage items?

Storage items can be: Cookie, Local Storage, or Session Storage. They can be grouped into a service or added to a category directly.

---

# How do I customize the consent banner?

## How do I show different banners by region?

Banners can be configured to show only in specific countries or U.S. states. This lets you create multiple banners customised for different regions and their regulations.

## How do I force users to consent before browsing?

Enable the force consent option to require users to accept/deny consent before interacting with the website.

## How do I change the banner layout?

The layout, position and button placement of banners can be customised. The preferences modal layout can also be customised.

## How do I customize banner buttons?

The weights, position and text of banner buttons can be customised.

> Some regulations like GDPR require banner buttons to be equally weighted.

## What is the Privacy Trigger?

A floating button that allows users to update their consent preferences at any time. Can be enabled/disabled and its position customised.

> Most regulations mandate that users must be able to update their consent preferences after giving their initial consent.

## How do I control what users see in preferences?

The preferences modal can show only categories, or include services and cookies for more granular control.

> Some regulations require all storage items and services to be shown.

## How do I theme the banner?

- Dark/Light mode - Choose between light, dark, or auto mode
- Colors - Customise primary, text, and button colors
- Border radius - Adjust corner roundness
- Logo - Add your company logo

For more detailed styling, use Custom CSS.

---

# How does CookieChimp handle multiple languages?

## How does language detection work?

CookieChimp automatically detects the language to display your consent banner in. The detection method is configured in banner settings:

1. **HTML document language** — the `lang` attribute on the `<html>` element (e.g. `<html lang="fr">`).
2. **Browser language** — the language set in the visitor's browser preferences.

The default language is used if the visitor's language is not supported.

## How do I override the detected language?

CookieChimp resolves the display language using this priority order:

1. `window.CookieChimpLang` — a global JavaScript variable you set before the CookieChimp script loads.
2. `data-lang` attribute on the `<html>` element.
3. The default detection method configured in your banner settings.

> The HTML `lang` attribute remains the recommended way to control the banner language, since it also benefits SEO and accessibility.

### Using window.CookieChimpLang

```html
<script>
  window.CookieChimpLang = "de";
</script>
```

### Using data-lang attribute

```html
<html lang="en" data-lang="de">
```

The language value must be a valid ISO 639-1 language code (e.g. `en`, `fr`, `de`, `es`) and you must have a translation available in your banner settings.

## How do I translate the banner?

Consent banners can be translated into multiple languages. AI auto translate is available for paid accounts. Categories, services and storage items can be individually translated.

---

# How do I use wildcard patterns for cookies?

Platforms that generate unique storage item names can create many individual cookies. Wildcards group these under a single pattern using `*`.

## How do wildcards work?

- Standard Naming: `session_12345`, `session_67890`, etc.
- Wildcard Usage: `session_*`

This matches any storage item of the same type that starts with `session_`.

---

# How do I block third-party iframes?

Block iframes from loading before consent and show a placeholder notice. Enable this feature in the CookieChimp dashboard.

## How do I block an iframe?

```html
<div data-service="youtube" data-id="<video-id>"></div>
```

## How do I customize the iframe placeholder?

```html
<div
  data-service="youtube"
  data-id="5b35haQV7tU"
  data-autoscale
  data-iframe-id="YoutubeEmbed"
  data-iframe-loading="lazy"
  data-iframe-frameborder="0"
></div>
```

### What configuration options are available?

- `data-service`: [String, Required] Service name (e.g., YouTube, Vimeo, Google Maps).
- `data-id`: [String, Required] Unique resource ID (e.g., video ID).
- `data-title`: [String] Notice title.
- `data-params`: [String] Iframe query parameters.
- `data-thumbnail`: [String] Path to custom thumbnail.
- `data-ratio`: [String] Custom aspect ratio (e.g., "16:9").
- `data-autoscale`: Responsive iframe that fills parent width.
- `data-widget`: Use for custom widgets with explicit width and height.

## Which services are supported?

- YouTube
- Vimeo
- Google Maps

---

# How do I identify users in consent records?

Match consent records with your application's user IDs. Add the following code to your HTML page, as high up in the `<head>` as possible (before CookieChimp loads):

```html
<script>
  window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
  window.dataLayer.push({
    userId: "your_user_ID_123", // Replace with your generated ID
  });
</script>
```

> Don't use any Personal Identifiable Information (PII) as the user ID. Use a unique ID that you can match with your database. PII will be discarded.

---

# How do I let users update their consent?

Most privacy regulations require that users can update their cookie consent preferences after initial consent.

## What is the Privacy Trigger?

CookieChimp provides a built-in floating privacy icon that allows users to manage preferences at any time. Enable and customize it in the Banner Designer settings.

## How do I add a custom consent button?

### Using data attributes

```html
<button type="button" data-cc="show-preferencesModal">
  Manage cookie preferences
</button>
```

Or as a link:

```html
<a href="#" data-cc="show-preferencesModal">
  Cookie Settings
</a>
```

### Using the JavaScript API

```html
<button type="button" onclick="CookieConsent.showPreferences()">
  Manage cookie preferences
</button>
```

## What are best practices for placement?

Place the link in an obvious location: website footer, privacy policy page, cookie policy page, or user account settings. Use clear, descriptive text like "Manage cookie preferences" or "Cookie settings". Make it accessible and keyboard navigable.

## What do regulations require?

Most privacy regulations mandate that users must be able to update their consent preferences. Satisfy this by enabling the Privacy Trigger or adding a custom button/link in an obvious location.

---

# How do I customize CookieChimp with CSS?

CookieChimp's dashboard allows you to customise the banner and modal. For more control, use custom CSS.

## How do I enable dark mode?

Add `cc--darkmode` class to the HTML element. Or enable auto dark mode in the CookieChimp dashboard.

## What CSS variables can I customize?

Override these CSS variables to build your own theme:

```css
:root {
  --cc-font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;
  --cc-modal-border-radius: 0.5rem;
  --cc-btn-border-radius: 0.4rem;
  --cc-modal-transition-duration: 0.25s;
  --cc-modal-margin: 1rem;
  --cc-z-index: 2147483647;
  --cc-bg: #ffffff;
  --cc-primary-color: #2c2f31;
  --cc-secondary-color: #5e6266;
  --cc-btn-primary-bg: #30363c;
  --cc-btn-primary-color: #ffffff;
  --cc-btn-primary-hover-bg: #000000;
  --cc-btn-primary-hover-color: #ffffff;
  --cc-btn-secondary-bg: #eaeff2;
  --cc-btn-secondary-color: var(--cc-primary-color);
  --cc-btn-secondary-hover-bg: #d4dae0;
  --cc-toggle-on-bg: var(--cc-btn-primary-bg);
  --cc-toggle-off-bg: #667481;
  --cc-overlay-bg: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.65);
  --cc-footer-bg: var(--cc-btn-secondary-bg);
  --cc-footer-color: var(--cc-secondary-color);
}
```

Dark mode variables are available under the `.cc--darkmode` class.

## How do I disable transitions?

```css
#cc-main {
  --cc-modal-transition-duration: 0;
}
```

---

# What callbacks and events does CookieChimp provide?

## What events can I listen for?

### Consent Events

**cc:onFirstConsent** - Triggered the first time user expresses their choice of consent.

```js
window.addEventListener("cc:onFirstConsent", function (event) {
  var detail = event.detail;
  // detail.cookie
});
```

**cc:onConsented** - Triggered on first consent and on every subsequent page load.

```js
window.addEventListener("cc:onConsented", function (event) {
  var detail = event.detail;
  // detail.cookie
});
```

**cc:onUpdate** - Triggered when the user modifies their preferences (only if consent has already been provided).

```js
window.addEventListener("cc:onUpdate", function (event) {
  var detail = event.detail;
  // detail.cookie
  // detail.changedCategories
  // detail.changedServices
});
```

### Modal Events

**cc:onModalShow** - The consent modal is visible.
**cc:onModalHide** - The consent modal is hidden.
**cc:onModalReady** - The consent modal is created and appended to the DOM.

All modal events provide `event.detail.modalName`.

## What actions can I call programmatically?

### show

Shows the consent banner. Pass `true` to generate it on the fly if consent was previously expressed.

```js
CookieChimp.show();
CookieChimp.show(true);
```

### hide

Hides the consent banner.

```js
CookieChimp.hide();
```

### showPreferences

Shows the preferences modal.

```js
CookieChimp.showPreferences();
```

### hidePreferences

Hides the preferences modal.

```js
CookieChimp.hidePreferences();
```

### getUserPreferences

Returns user's preferences.

```typescript
function(): {
    acceptType: string,          // 'all', 'custom', or 'necessary'
    acceptedCategories: string[],
    rejectedCategories: string[],
    acceptedServices: {[category: string]: string[]}
    rejectedServices: {[category: string]: string[]}
}
```

```javascript
var preferences = CookieChimp.getUserPreferences();

if (preferences.acceptType === "all") {
  console.log("Everything has been accepted!");
}
```

### acceptCategory

Programmatically accept or reject a cookie category.

```js
CookieChimp.acceptCategory("all");             // accept all
CookieChimp.acceptCategory([]);                // reject all (keep necessary)
CookieChimp.acceptCategory();                  // accept current modal selection
CookieChimp.acceptCategory("analytics");       // accept one category
CookieChimp.acceptCategory(["analytics", "marketing"]); // accept specific categories
CookieChimp.acceptCategory("all", ["analytics"]);       // accept all except one
```

### acceptedCategory

Returns `true` if the specified category was accepted.

```javascript
if (CookieChimp.acceptedCategory("analytics")) {
  // "analytics" category was accepted
}
```

### acceptService

Accepts or rejects services.

```javascript
CookieChimp.acceptService("all", "analytics");           // accept all in category
CookieChimp.acceptService([], "analytics");               // reject all in category
CookieChimp.acceptService("Google Analytics", "analytics"); // accept one service
```

### acceptedService

Returns `true` if the specified service was accepted.

```javascript
if (CookieChimp.acceptedService("Google Analytics", "analytics")) {
  // "Google Analytics" service was accepted
}
```

### validConsent

Returns `true` if consent is valid. Consent is NOT valid when: consent is missing, CookieChimp's cookie doesn't exist/has expired, or cookie is structurally invalid.

```javascript
if (CookieChimp.validConsent()) {
  // consent is valid
}
```

### reset

Resets CookieChimp. Pass `true` to delete the consent cookie. The page must be reloaded after calling this.

```js
CookieChimp.reset(true);
window.location.reload();
```
